basic_details.job_title: APGENCO AEE Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 100 Assistant Executive Engineer Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Andhra Pradesh Power Generation Corporation Limited (APGENCO) has released the APGENCO AEE Recruitment 2026 notification (No. 01/CGM(Adm, IS&ERP)/2026, dated 25 June 2026) for 100 vacancies of Assistant Executive Engineer (AEE) across Electrical, Mechanical, Electronics, and Civil branches. Engineering graduates aged 18 to 42 years (as on 1 July 2026) can apply online from 30 June 2026 to 20 July 2026 at apgenco.gov.in or apvidyutrecruitment.apcfss.in. Selected candidates will receive a consolidated monthly remuneration of ₹75,000/- during the 2-year probation period, after which the regul

application_fee.details: The APGENCO AEE 2026 application fee is ₹1,000/- for all candidates, payable through the online Payment Gateway between 30 June 2026 and 20 July 2026. This fee is towards the processing and conducting of the Common Recruitment Test. Candidates should note that the fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances. On successful fee payment, a 'Journal Number' will be issued to proceed with the online application submission. | Note: Receipt of the Journal Number only confirms payment of fee. It does not mean the online application submission is complete. Candidates must proceed to fill and submit the full application form after fee payment.

age_limit.details: To be eligible for the APGENCO Assistant Executive Engineer Recruitment 2026, candidates must hold a B.E./B.Tech./A.M.I.E. degree in the relevant engineering discipline from a recognized university in India. All educational qualifications must be completed as on the date of issue of the notification (25 June 2026). Candidates still appearing in their final exams are not eligible. | Important Notes on Qualification: | Only qualifications mentioned by the candidate in the application form will be considered for selection. If claiming an equivalent qualification, an Equivalency Certificate from the Registrar of the concerned University or Secretary of the concerned Institute, or a copy of the relevant Government Order, must be produced. Only degrees recognized by UGC and AICTE (as applicable) will be considered. Degrees awarded under Distance Mode through study centers of other State Universities (beyond Andhra Pradesh) are not legally valid.

All educational qualifications must be completed as on the date of issue of the notification (25 June 2026) . Candidates still appearing in their final exams are not eligible.

salary_details.details: Candidates selected under the APGENCO AEE Recruitment 2026 will be placed on probation for 2 years (within a continuous period of 3 years) during which they receive a consolidated monthly remuneration of ₹75,000/-. After successful completion of probation, the regular pay scale under RPS-2022 applies, along with multiple allowances. The posts also come with valuable non-monetary benefits including departmental quarters, hospital facilities, and DAV school access at generating stations. | Accommodation: Departmental quarters provided at generating stations Medical: Hospital facilities at respective generating stations Education: DAV School facility at all generating stations | Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

selection_process: The APGENCO AEE 2026 selection process is entirely merit-based through a Computer Based Test (CBT) of 100 marks and 3 hours duration. There is no interview or personality test. After the CBT
candidates shortlisted in a 1:1 ratio against notified vacancies are called for Certificate Verification
following which the final selection list is prepared and allotments are made to respective utilities and zones.
Duration: 3 hours Medium: English only Negative Marking: None Normalization: A normalization formula will be applied since the exam is conducted in multiple shifts across multiple days

Note: Merely securing minimum qualifying marks does not vest any right to a candidate for being called for Certificate Verification. No TA/DA is payable for appearing in the CBT or for Certificate Verification.
Application & Fee Payment (by 20 July 2026): Submit online application and pay ₹1

000/- fee. Hall Ticket Download: Hall tickets available for download 7 days before the exam date. Mock Test: Online mock tests available from 15 August 2026 to help candidates get familiar with the CBT format. Computer Based Test (CBT): Conducted from 22 to 29 August 2026 in 52 cities across AP and select cities in other states. Initial Answer Key Release: Published on the 2nd day after the last exam; candidates can file objections within 7 days. Final Answer Key: Released after 7 days from the last date for receiving objections. Merit List (Marks) Announcement: Published 7 days after the final key release (tentatively September last week). Certificate Verification: Candidates shortlisted in 1:1 ratio called for document verification. Provisional Selection List: Published on official website after successful certificate verification. Allotment to Utility & Zone: Based on merit

and preferences exercised in application. Induction Training (3 months): All selected candidates undergo induction training at Training Institute/Dr. NTTPS before posting. Appointment & Probation: 2-year probation with ₹75
000/- consolidated remuneration; regular scale applies after probation.

syllabus.details: The APGENCO AEE CBT 2026 consists of 70 marks from core branch-specific topics and 30 marks from common sections. The common sections cover Reasoning, General Awareness, English, Quantitative Aptitude (up to 10+2 level), and Computer Knowledge. The core syllabus is technical and covers degree-level engineering subjects specific to each branch. | Electric Circuits, Electrical Machines (transformers, DC machines, induction motors, synchronous machines), Power Systems (generation, transmission, protection, stability), Utilization & Control Systems (feedback, Bode plots, traction), Measurements (bridges, instruments), Analog & Digital Electronics, and Power Electronics & Drives. | Strength of Materials, Fluid Mechanics & Machinery, Material Science & Metallurgy, Theory of Machines, Vibrations, Design of Machine Elements, Heat Transfer, Thermodynamics, Applied Thermodynamics, Turbo Machines, Metal Cutting & Machine Tools, Foundry/Welding/Forging, and Production & Operation Management. | Basics of Circuits & Measurement Systems, Transducers & Industrial Instrumentation, Analog Electronics, Digital Electronics, Signals/Systems/Communications, Electrical & Electronic Measurements, Control Systems & Process Control, and Analytical & Optical Instrumentation. | Strength of Materials, Reinforced Concrete (design of beams, slabs, columns, footings), Soil Mechanics, Steel Structures, Fluid Mechanics & Machinery, and Foundation Engineering.

how_to_apply: The APGENCO AEE Apply Online 2026 process is available at apgenco.gov.in or apvidyutrecruitment.apcfss.in. Applications sent through any other mode will not be entertained. Candidates must complete registration, fee payment, application filling, and document upload - all before 20 July 2026 at 11:59 PM. Certificates not uploaded by the deadline will result in non-generation of hall tickets.
Visit the official portal: Go to https://apgenco.gov.in or https://apvidyutrecruitment.apcfss.in.

Register using Aadhaar-linked mobile number: Click the registration link, enter your details, and complete OTP verification sent to your Aadhaar-linked mobile number. Save your Registration ID and password.
Pay the application fee: Pay ₹1,000/- through the Payment Gateway. Note down the Journal Number issued on successful payment. Journal Number alone does not mean application is submitted.

Log in and fill the application form: Use your Registration ID and password to log in. Fill all required fields - educational qualifications, zone preferences, academic details, etc. Do not leave any relevant column blank.

Exercise preferences: Select your preferred Power Utility (for Electrical/Civil: APGENCO, APTRANSCO, APEPDCL, APCPDCL, APSPDCL) and preferred Zone/Unit. These preferences are final and cannot be changed after submission.

Upload scanned documents: Upload a recent passport-size photograph, scanned signature, and all required certificates (community, EWS, PwBD/SADAREM, qualification, study/residence certificates, etc.). All documents must be clear, legible scanned copies.
Review and submit: Verify all entries carefully before final submission. Incomplete or incorrect applications will be summarily rejected.
Print confirmation: Save or print the submitted application form for future reference.

Important: Candidates currently in government/autonomous/aided institution service must inform their Head of Office in writing that they are applying for this recruitment and must furnish a No Objection Certificate while submitting the online application.

content_sections.heading: APGENCO AEE Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF Download
content_sections.text: The complete APGENCO AEE Recruitment 2026 official notification PDF (Notification No. 01/CGM(Adm, IS&ERP)/2026, dated 25 June 2026) is a 29-page document available on the official website. It contains the full vacancy tables (Annexure I & II), branch-wise and community-wise breakdowns, zone-wise generating station details, detailed syllabus for all branches, normalization formula, service bond conditions, and the complete recruitment schedule. A step-by-step User Guide for online application is also available on the portal from 25 June 2026.

content_sections.heading: Important Dates - APGENCO AEE Recruitment 2026 Schedule
content_sections.text: Candidates interested in the APGENCO AEE Vacancy 2026 must be aware of all critical dates to avoid missing out. The online application portal opens on 30 June 2026 at 10:00 AM and closes on 20 July 2026 at 11:59 PM - this is also the deadline for fee payment and uploading all required certificates. The Computer Based Test (CBT) is scheduled from 22 August to 29 August 2026 , with hall tickets available 7 days before the exam.

content_sections.heading: APGENCO AEE Vacancy 2026 - Branch-wise and Zone-wise Breakdown
content_sections.text: The APGENCO AEE Recruitment 2026 notification covers a total of 100 provisional vacancies across four engineering branches - Electrical (52), Mechanical (21), Electronics (7), and Civil (20). Vacancies are distributed across Zones 2 to 6 of Andhra Pradesh; Zone 1 has no vacancies in any branch. All vacancies are provisional and may increase or decrease based on administrative requirements and government directions. A Common Recruitment Examination is conducted jointly by APGENCO, APTRANSCO, APEPDCL, APCPDCL, and APSPDCL for the Electrical and Civil branches. Candidates selected through this common exam are allotted to respective utilities based on merit, reservation, and preferences. A separate examination is conducted exclusively for the Mechanical and Electronics branches in APGENCO. APTRANSCO AEE Recruitment 2026 Notification Out for 200 Assistant Executive Engineer Posts

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria - Qualification & Age Limit for APGENCO AEE 2026
content_sections.text: To be eligible for the APGENCO Assistant Executive Engineer Recruitment 2026 , candidates must hold a B.E./B.Tech./A.M.I.E. degree in the relevant engineering discipline from a recognized university in India. All educational qualifications must be completed as on the date of issue of the notification (25 June 2026) . Candidates still appearing in their final exams are not eligible. Important Notes on Qualification: Only qualifications mentioned by the candidate in the application form will be considered for selection. If claiming an equivalent qualification, an Equivalency Certificate from the Registrar of the concerned University or Secretary of the concerned Institute, or a copy of the relevant Government Order, must be produced. Only degrees recognized by UGC and AICTE (as applicable) will be considered. Degrees awarded under Distance Mode through study centers of other State Universities (beyond Andhra Pradesh) are not legally valid.

rows.required_qualification: B.E. / B.Tech . / A.M.I.E. in Electrical & Electronics Engineering or equivalent from any recognized University in India
B.E. / B.Tech. / A.M.I.E. in Mechanical Engineering or equivalent from any recognized University in India

B.E. / B.Tech. / A.M.I.E. in Electronics & Communication Engineering / Instrumentation & Controls Engineering / Electronics & Instrumentation Engineering / Electronics & Control Engineering / Instrumentation Engineering / Electronics Instrumentation & Power / Power Electronics / Computer Science Engineering / Computer Science & Information Technology or equivalent
B.E. / B.Tech. / A.M.I.E. in Civil Engineering or equivalent from any recognized University in India

content_sections.heading: Application Fee - APGENCO AEE Exam 2026
content_sections.text: The APGENCO AEE 2026 application fee is ₹1,000/- for all candidates, payable through the online Payment Gateway between 30 June 2026 and 20 July 2026 . This fee is towards the processing and conducting of the Common Recruitment Test. Candidates should note that the fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances. On successful fee payment, a 'Journal Number' will be issued to proceed with the online application submission. Note: Receipt of the Journal Number only confirms payment of fee. It does not mean the online application submission is complete. Candidates must proceed to fill and submit the full application form after fee payment.

rows.category: All Categories (General / OBC / SC / ST / EWS / PwBD)

content_sections.heading: Selection Process - APGENCO AEE Exam Pattern and CBT Details 2026
content_sections.text: The APGENCO AEE 2026 selection process is entirely merit-based through a Computer Based Test (CBT) of 100 marks and 3 hours duration. There is no interview or personality test. After the CBT, candidates shortlisted in a 1:1 ratio against notified vacancies are called for Certificate Verification, following which the final selection list is prepared and allotments are made to respective utilities and zones. Duration: 3 hours Medium: English only Negative Marking: None Normalization: A normalization formula will be applied since the exam is conducted in multiple shifts across multiple days Note: Merely securing minimum qualifying marks does not vest any right to a candidate for being called for Certificate Verification. No TA/DA is payable for appearing in the CBT or for Certificate Verification. Application & Fee Payment (by 20 July 2026): Submit online application and pay ₹1,000/- fee. Hall Ticket Download: Hall tickets available for download 7 days before the exam date. Mock Test: Online mock tests available from 15 August 2026 to help candidates get familiar with the CBT format. Computer Based Test (CBT): Conducted from 22 to 29 August 2026 in 52 cities across AP and select cities in other states. Initial Answer Key Release: Published on the 2nd day after the last exam; candidates can file objections within 7 days. Final Answer Key: Released after 7 days from the last date for receiving objections. Merit List (Marks) Announcement: Published 7 days after the final key release (tentatively September last week). Certificate Verification: Candidates shortlisted in 1:1 ratio called for document verification. Provisional Selection List: Published on official website after successful certificate verification. Allotment to Utility & Zone: Based on merit, reservation, local candidature, and preferences exercised in application. Induction Training (3 months): All selected candidates undergo induction training at Training Institute/Dr. NTTPS before posting. Appointment & Probation: 2-year probation with ₹75,000/- consolidated remuneration; regular scale applies after probation.

content_sections.heading: APGENCO AEE Apply Online 2026 - Step-by-Step Application Process
content_sections.text: The APGENCO AEE Apply Online 2026 process is available at apgenco.gov.in or apvidyutrecruitment.apcfss.in . Applications sent through any other mode will not be entertained. Candidates must complete registration, fee payment, application filling, and document upload - all before 20 July 2026 at 11:59 PM . Certificates not uploaded by the deadline will result in non-generation of hall tickets. Visit the official portal: Go to https://apgenco.gov.in or https://apvidyutrecruitment.apcfss.in . Register using Aadhaar-linked mobile number: Click the registration link, enter your details, and complete OTP verification sent to your Aadhaar-linked mobile number. Save your Registration ID and password. Pay the application fee: Pay ₹1,000/- through the Payment Gateway. Note down the Journal Number issued on successful payment. Journal Number alone does not mean application is submitted. Log in and fill the application form: Use your Registration ID and password to log in. Fill all required fields - educational qualifications, zone preferences, academic details, etc. Do not leave any relevant column blank. Exercise preferences: Select your preferred Power Utility (for Electrical/Civil: APGENCO, APTRANSCO, APEPDCL, APCPDCL, APSPDCL) and preferred Zone/Unit. These preferences are final and cannot be changed after submission. Upload scanned documents: Upload a recent passport-size photograph, scanned signature, and all required certificates (community, EWS, PwBD/SADAREM, qualification, study/residence certificates, etc.). All documents must be clear, legible scanned copies. Review and submit: Verify all entries carefully before final submission. Incomplete or incorrect applications will be summarily rejected. Print confirmation: Save or print the submitted application form for future reference. Important: Candidates currently in government/autonomous/aided institution service must inform their Head of Office in writing that they are applying for this recruitment and must furnish a No Objection Certificate while submitting the online application.

content_sections.heading: Salary and Pay Scale - APGENCO AEE 2026 Remuneration Details
content_sections.text: Candidates selected under the APGENCO AEE Recruitment 2026 will be placed on probation for 2 years (within a continuous period of 3 years) during which they receive a consolidated monthly remuneration of ₹75,000/- . After successful completion of probation, the regular pay scale under RPS-2022 applies, along with multiple allowances. The posts also come with valuable non-monetary benefits including departmental quarters, hospital facilities, and DAV school access at generating stations. Accommodation: Departmental quarters provided at generating stations Medical: Hospital facilities at respective generating stations Education: DAV School facility at all generating stations Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

content_sections.heading: Service Bond & Deposit Conditions - APGENCO AEE Appointment 2026
content_sections.text: Every candidate appointed under the APGENCO AEE 2026 notification must execute a Service Bond at the time of joining. The bond requires the candidate to serve APGENCO for a minimum of 5 years after completion of the 2-year probation period - effectively a total commitment of 7 years . During this entire 7-year bond period, original certificates (degree, date of birth, community, etc.) must be deposited with APGENCO. Service Bond must be executed on a Non-Judicial Stamp Paper worth ₹110/- at the time of joining. Original certificates (Degree, DOB, Community) deposited with APGENCO for the entire 7-year bond period. If certificates are needed during the bond period for a bonafide purpose, a security deposit of ₹1,00,000/- (refundable) must be paid before obtaining them. Candidates leaving service during the 2-year probation must refund all emoluments received plus ₹5,00,000/- as liquidated damages. Candidates resigning without completing 5 years after probation must pay ₹10,00,000/- as liquidated damages.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required - Certificate Upload & Verification Checklist
content_sections.text: All required documents must be uploaded at the time of submitting the online application for APGENCO AEE Vacancy 2026 . Unless certificates are uploaded before the last date, hall tickets will not be generated. At the Certificate Verification stage, original documents must be produced. Illegible or tampered documents will lead to rejection of candidature. Application form and Hall Ticket Educational qualification certificates (SSC, Intermediate, B.E./B.Tech. Degree, etc.) Proof of Age (Birth Certificate or SSC Memo) Original Community Certificate (for SC/ST/BC/EWS candidates) issued by competent authority SADAREM Certificate (for PwBD candidates) - issued by competent authority School Study Certificates (for local candidature claim) Residence Certificate from competent authority (if candidate has not studied prescribed qualification in any educational institution) EWS Income Certificate for the financial year prior to the year of application, issued by competent authority Proof of Identity: Aadhaar Card / Voter ID / PAN Card / Passport / Driving License / Service ID Card / Bank Passbook with photo No Objection Certificate from Employer (for candidates in government/autonomous/aided service) Local Status Certificate for candidates who migrated from Telangana to Andhra Pradesh between 2 June 2014 and 1 June 2024 Equivalency Certificate (if qualification claimed is equivalent to prescribed qualification)

content_sections.heading: APGENCO AEE 2026 Exam Syllabus - Branch-wise Key Topics
content_sections.text: The APGENCO AEE CBT 2026 consists of 70 marks from core branch-specific topics and 30 marks from common sections. The common sections cover Reasoning, General Awareness, English, Quantitative Aptitude (up to 10+2 level), and Computer Knowledge. The core syllabus is technical and covers degree-level engineering subjects specific to each branch. Electric Circuits, Electrical Machines (transformers, DC machines, induction motors, synchronous machines), Power Systems (generation, transmission, protection, stability), Utilization & Control Systems (feedback, Bode plots, traction), Measurements (bridges, instruments), Analog & Digital Electronics, and Power Electronics & Drives. Strength of Materials, Fluid Mechanics & Machinery, Material Science & Metallurgy, Theory of Machines, Vibrations, Design of Machine Elements, Heat Transfer, Thermodynamics, Applied Thermodynamics, Turbo Machines, Metal Cutting & Machine Tools, Foundry/Welding/Forging, and Production & Operation Management. Basics of Circuits & Measurement Systems, Transducers & Industrial Instrumentation, Analog Electronics, Digital Electronics, Signals/Systems/Communications, Electrical & Electronic Measurements, Control Systems & Process Control, and Analytical & Optical Instrumentation. Strength of Materials, Reinforced Concrete (design of beams, slabs, columns, footings), Soil Mechanics, Steel Structures, Fluid Mechanics & Machinery, and Foundation Engineering.

rows.key_topics: Analogy, Classification, Series, Coding/Decoding, Venn Diagrams, Critical Thinking
Current Events, History, Culture, Geography, Economy, Scientific Research (India & Neighbours)
Grammar, Vocabulary, Comprehension, Active/Passive Voice, Idioms, Error Spotting
Number Systems, Percentages, Profit/Loss, Interest, Work & Time, Speed & Distance (10+2 level)
Computer Basics, MS Office, Internet & Email, Networking, Cybersecurity Basics

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid - APGENCO AEE Application Troubleshooting 2026
content_sections.text: Many candidates miss out on the APGENCO AEE Exam 2026 due to avoidable errors during the application process. Here are the most critical mistakes to watch out for: Confusing Journal Number with completed application: Paying the fee and receiving a Journal Number does NOT mean the application is submitted. Candidates must log in and complete the full application form after fee payment. Not uploading certificates before the deadline: All certificates must be uploaded by 20 July 2026. Without uploaded certificates, the hall ticket will not be generated - candidates cannot appear for the exam. Using Distance Mode degrees from other State Universities: Degrees awarded under Distance Mode through study centers of universities outside Andhra Pradesh are not legally valid for this recruitment. Apply only with qualifications recognized by UGC/AICTE. Not noting that zone and utility preferences are final: Preferences for Power Utility (for Electrical/Civil branches) and Zone/Unit submitted in the online application cannot be changed under any circumstances after submission. Choose carefully before clicking "Submit." Attempting to bring mobile phones or electronic devices to exam: Mobile phones, calculators, smart watches, Bluetooth devices, and all electronic storage/communication devices are strictly prohibited at the exam centre - even in switched-off mode. Violations attract a ban from future exams. Not arriving 1 hour before exam time: Candidates must report to the examination centre at least 1 hour before commencement for biometric verification. No candidate is permitted to enter after the prescribed reporting time mentioned on the hall ticket. Not carrying government-issued photo ID to the exam: Apart from the hall ticket, candidates must carry at least one original valid government-issued photo ID (Aadhaar, Voter ID, PAN Card, Passport, or Driving License). Candidates without valid ID are not permitted to appear. Exercising external influence or canvassing: Any attempt to influence the recruitment process through recommendations, political pressure, or third-party canvassing at any stage results in permanent disqualification not just from this but from any future APGENCO recruitment. Leaving the application form incomplete: No relevant column in the application form should be left blank - the application will not be accepted if any required field is empty. Once submitted, no additional information furnished later will be entertained. Not factoring in the 7-year bond commitment: Candidates should carefully consider the service bond requirements (5 years after 2-year probation = 7 years total) and the liquidated damages clauses (₹5 lakh if leaving during probation; ₹10 lakh if leaving before completing 5 years post-probation) before applying.

content_sections.heading: Reservation Policy - APGENCO AEE Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: The APGENCO AEE 2026 reservation follows vertical reservations for SC (Group-I, II & III), ST, BC (A, B, C, D & E), and EWS candidates as per applicable Andhra Pradesh Government Orders. Horizontal reservations of 33⅓% for Women and 4% for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities are implemented within respective vertical categories. Candidates from states other than Andhra Pradesh will be considered under the General (unreserved) category only. Local candidature (95% of vacancies) is reserved for candidates from the respective zone as per the Presidential Order 2025. Open (non-local) vacancies constitute 5% of total vacancies in each branch. PwBD candidates must hold a valid SADAREM certificate issued by a competent authority. For PwBD category (d) - Autism, Intellectual Disability, Specific Learning Disability, Mental Illness - vacancies will be filled with PwBD category (c) - Locomotor disability including cerebral palsy, leprosy cured, dwarfism, acid attack victims, and muscular dystrophy.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for APGENCO AEE 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application, pay the fee of ₹1,000/-, and upload all required certificates is 20 July 2026 at 11:59 PM. Applications submitted or fees paid after this deadline will not be entertained.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in APGENCO AEE Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 100 provisional vacancies are available — 52 for Electrical, 21 for Mechanical, 7 for Electronics, and 20 for Civil branch. These numbers may increase or decrease based on administrative requirements.

faq.question: Q3. What is the salary for APGENCO AEE 2026 selected candidates?
faq.answer: During the 2-year probation period, selected candidates receive a consolidated monthly remuneration of ₹75,000/-. After successful completion of probation, the regular pay scale under RPS-2022 starting at ₹86,245/- applies, along with Project Allowance, Shift Allowance, Conveyance Allowance, Hotline Allowance, and PLGA.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for APGENCO AEE 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is ₹1,000/- for all candidates, regardless of category. The fee is non-refundable and must be paid through the online Payment Gateway between 30 June 2026 and 20 July 2026.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for APGENCO AEE Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 18 and 42 years of age as on 1 July 2026. SC/ST/BC candidates of Andhra Pradesh get a 5-year relaxation (maximum age 47 years). Persons with Benchmark Disabilities get a 10-year relaxation (maximum age 52 years).

faq.question: Q6. Is there an interview for APGENCO AEE 2026 selection?
faq.answer: No. The selection is entirely based on the Computer Based Test (CBT) of 100 marks and 3 hours duration. There is no separate interview. After the CBT merit list, candidates shortlisted in a 1:1 ratio are called for Certificate Verification, following which the final selection list is prepared.

faq.question: Q7. What is the APGENCO AEE 2026 exam date?
faq.answer: The Computer Based Test (CBT) is scheduled from 22 August 2026 to 29 August 2026. Hall tickets will be available for download 7 days before the commencement of the examination from the official website.

faq.question: Q8. Is there negative marking in the APGENCO AEE 2026 CBT?
faq.answer: No, there is no negative marking in the Computer Based Test. Each question is worth marks as per the distribution, and wrong answers do not attract any penalty.

faq.question: Q9. What is the service bond requirement for APGENCO AEE 2026?
faq.answer: Selected candidates must execute a service bond at joining to serve APGENCO for a minimum of 5 years after completion of the 2-year probation (total 7 years). If leaving during probation, ₹5 lakh is payable as liquidated damages. If leaving before completing 5 years after probation, ₹10 lakh is payable. Original certificates are deposited with APGENCO for the entire bond period.

faq.question: Q10. What is the official website for APGENCO AEE Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The official websites are https://apgenco.gov.in and https://apvidyutrecruitment.apcfss.in. All notifications, hall tickets, answer keys, merit lists, and updates are published on these websites. The helpdesk is available from 10:00 AM to 5:30 PM on all working days.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for APGENCO AEE 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application, pay the fee of ₹1,000/-, and upload all required certificates is 20 July 2026 at 11:59 PM. Applications submitted or fees paid after this deadline will not be entertained.

faq.question: How many vacancies are available in APGENCO AEE Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 100 provisional vacancies are available — 52 for Electrical, 21 for Mechanical, 7 for Electronics, and 20 for Civil branch. These numbers may increase or decrease based on administrative requirements.

faq.question: What is the salary for APGENCO AEE 2026 selected candidates?
faq.answer: During the 2-year probation period, selected candidates receive a consolidated monthly remuneration of ₹75,000/-. After successful completion of probation, the regular pay scale under RPS-2022 starting at ₹86,245/- applies, along with Project Allowance, Shift Allowance, Conveyance Allowance, Hotline Allowance, and PLGA.

faq.question: What is the application fee for APGENCO AEE 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is ₹1,000/- for all candidates, regardless of category. The fee is non-refundable and must be paid through the online Payment Gateway between 30 June 2026 and 20 July 2026.

faq.question: What is the age limit for APGENCO AEE Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 18 and 42 years of age as on 1 July 2026. SC/ST/BC candidates of Andhra Pradesh get a 5-year relaxation (maximum age 47 years). Persons with Benchmark Disabilities get a 10-year relaxation (maximum age 52 years).

faq.question: Is there an interview for APGENCO AEE 2026 selection?
faq.answer: No. The selection is entirely based on the Computer Based Test (CBT) of 100 marks and 3 hours duration. There is no separate interview. After the CBT merit list, candidates shortlisted in a 1:1 ratio are called for Certificate Verification, following which the final selection list is prepared.

faq.question: What is the APGENCO AEE 2026 exam date?
faq.answer: The Computer Based Test (CBT) is scheduled from 22 August 2026 to 29 August 2026. Hall tickets will be available for download 7 days before the commencement of the examination from the official website.

faq.question: Is there negative marking in the APGENCO AEE 2026 CBT?
faq.answer: No, there is no negative marking in the Computer Based Test. Each question is worth marks as per the distribution, and wrong answers do not attract any penalty.

faq.question: What is the service bond requirement for APGENCO AEE 2026?
faq.answer: Selected candidates must execute a service bond at joining to serve APGENCO for a minimum of 5 years after completion of the 2-year probation (total 7 years). If leaving during probation, ₹5 lakh is payable as liquidated damages. If leaving before completing 5 years after probation, ₹10 lakh is payable. Original certificates are deposited with APGENCO for the entire bond period.

faq.question: What is the official website for APGENCO AEE Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The official websites are https://apgenco.gov.in and https://apvidyutrecruitment.apcfss.in. All notifications, hall tickets, answer keys, merit lists, and updates are published on these websites. The helpdesk is available from 10:00 AM to 5:30 PM on all working days.
